creator: Theresa Loconsolo
title: (No AI) What happens when companies become too AI-pilled?
dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
content: The people deciding that AI can replace your job are also the ones least likely to understand what your job truly involves, according to Box founder Aaron Levie, who pointed to this as an example of “AI psychosis.” Indeed, ClickUp recently cut 22% of its workforce for AI agents, tech layoffs in 2026 are already nearly matching all of 2025, \[…\]

creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
title: (No AI) After Nvidia’s $20B not-aqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
content: Chipmaker Groq is looking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it pivots from hardware to focus more on AI inference, the process of refining the way AI models respond to prompted requests, per Axios.
contentSnippet: Chipmaker Groq is looking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it pivots from hardware to focus more on AI inference, the process of refining the way AI models respond to prompted requests, per Axios.

title: (No AI) Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/STK414_AI_CVIRGINIA_I__0009_7.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

This week, an AI training startup called Shift [said it would clean New Yorkers' homes for free](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/939765/ai-training-data-startup-shift-free-cleaning). It has plans to expand into other cities as well, including London, and looking around my flat, I get the appeal.

But there's a catch. There's always a catch.

In exchange for the cleaning, Shift wants footage of its cleaners at work: scrubbing dishes, wiping counters, dusting tables, mopping floors. It wants everything. Video of all the boring domestic labor we'd happily outsource if we could - and that robotics companies are racing to teach machines to do so they can sell us something to do it for us.

That's harder than it sounds. Unlike cha …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/940007/ai-companies-will-pay-for-robot-training-data)

Video of all the boring domestic labor we'd happily outsource if we could - and that robotics companies are racing to teach machines to do so they can sell us something to do it for us.
That's harder than it sounds. Unlike cha …

title: (No AI) Jony Ive’s funky Ferrari
author: David Pierce
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/VRG_VST_052926_Site.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Most people will never own, drive, or even sit inside [a Ferrari Luce](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/937066/ferrari-luce-ev-jony-ive-marc-newson-lovefrom). (If you can, or do… hit us up.) There's still no question that Ferrari's first electric vehicle is one of the most interesting, surprising cars of the year. With [a decidedly un-Ferrari look](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/937077/ferrari-luce-ev-apple-car-jony-ive-design), and lots of new technology and designs courtesy of Sir Jony Ive, the Luce is a lot of big ideas in a single swoopy package. A lot of people [really hate it](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/938016/this-ferrari-should-have-been-a-volkswagen).

![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/11/Vergecast-Tile-Large.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

_Verge_ subscribers, don't forget you get exclusive access to ad-free _Vergecast_ wherever you get your podcasts. Head [here](https://www.theverge.com/account/podcasts). Not a subscriber? You can [sign up here](https://www.theverge.com/subscribe).

On [this episode of _The Vergecast_](https://pod.link/vergecast), we talk a lot about the Luce. D …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/939589/ferrari-luce-jony-ive-vergecast)

title: (No AI) Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
content: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to improve patient care, reduce operational burden, and help diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases.
contentSnippet: Boston Children’s Hospital uses OpenAI technology to improve patient care, reduce operational burden, and help diagnose more than 40 rare disease cases.

title: (No AI) Strengthening societal resilience with Rosalind Biodefense
content: OpenAI launches Rosalind Biodefense, expanding trusted access to GPT-Rosalind for vetted developers and U.S. government partners advancing biodefense, public health, and pandemic preparedness through frontier AI.
contentSnippet: OpenAI launches Rosalind Biodefense, expanding trusted access to GPT-Rosalind for vetted developers and U.S. government partners advancing biodefense, public health, and pandemic preparedness through frontier AI.

title: (No AI) We’re launching the Google DeepMind Accelerator program in Asia Pacific to tackle environmental risks
(No AI) Fast-tracking genetic leads to reverse cellular aging
content: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.
contentSnippet: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.

creator: Kate Knibbs
title: (No AI) We Asked the ‘Future of Truth’ Author to Explain How He Used AI. It Didn’t Go Well
dc:creator: Kate Knibbs
content: A book about how AI shapes perceptions of reality came under fire for using AI-generated quotes. Its problems go beyond that.
contentSnippet: A book about how AI shapes perceptions of reality came under fire for using AI-generated quotes. Its problems go beyond that.

creator: Steven Levy
title: (No AI) The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic
dc:creator: Steven Levy
content: Pope Leo XIV may not be able to disarm AI, but he’s got the attention of the industry.
